verification.notes: All three NEW files are sorry-free. Existing sorries in MultiFamilyBFMCS.lean remain but are documented as mathematically necessary blockers.

artifacts.summary: Witness obligation tracking and construction infrastructure
Multi-flag closure construction for modal saturation
MCS-level saturation and modal forward proof
Implementation summary documenting what was achieved and why sorries remain

review_reason: The original sorries in MultiFamilyBFMCS.lean (modal_backward) cannot be eliminated with the singleton approach - this is mathematically proven impossible. The task created sorry-free infrastructure for the correct multi-family approach, but full resolution requires architectural changes to BFMCS (heterogeneous domains or families with different mcs functions).

partial_progress.details: Created sorry-free infrastructure (3 new files, ~580 lines). Key theorem: closedFlags_closed_under_witnesses proves MCS-level saturation. MultiFamilyBFMCS sorries documented as mathematically necessary blockers.

metadata.blocker_analysis: Singleton BFMCS approach is mathematically impossible. Diamond(psi) in t.world does NOT imply psi in t.world. Counterexample: {Diamond(p), neg(p)} is consistent. Multi-family with different mcs functions is required.
